ref: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
text: Yesu ɔsa manyɛse ma nto manyɛ sinyɛ agbãagbã fiɛ ɔsa siwarã lalaa ɔɖi i mma ame sipia. Ɔ̃itã siɖe ika alasɔ siɣe sɔ ɔ̃ nɖe Kristo. Yesu Ɔɣɛ Ɣaa Itɔ̃me I Galilea Karɔ̃ Iso.
translated: The angel appeared to Joseph and said, 'Do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, for the child she has conceived is of the Holy Spirit. She will give birth to a son and you will name him Jesus, for he will save his people from their sins.' Now all this took place to fulfill what had been spoken by the Lord through the prophet: 'The virgin shall be with child and shall bring forth a son, and they shall name him Emmanuel,' which is translated, 'God is with us.' And Joseph arose from his sleep and did as the angel of the Lord had commanded him: he took his wife, but had no marital relations with her until she had brought forth a son; and he named him Jesus. Now when Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of King Herod, behold, wise men from the East came to Jerusalem

ref: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
text: Nto loɣere-ɔ sɔ Ɣaa to ɔ̃anyɔ Sodom nnya ɔɖo-ɔ ku atɔ̃me iɣɛyi ɔɖo mi.” Yesu Ɔɣere Maturi Sɔ Maana Iwarã I Ɔ̃ Kɔrɛ.
translated: “Go and preach to the people of Sodom about the punishment that will come upon them.” Jesus Christ said to his disciple.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Ɔbiara gɔ loabie sɔ ɔ̃akɔkɔ ɔ̃ ngbã to ɔ̃ayura me, ɣɛɛ ɔbiara gɔ loasu ɔ̃ ngbã ɔtã ala wũ ɔso to ɔ̃ana ngbã. Ɣaa To Ɔ̃atã Ɔbiara Gɔ Lofɔ Yesu Masiaidze Kuso.
translated: Everyone who does not love does not know God, because God is love. This is how God showed his love among us: He sent his one and only Son into the world that we might live through him. This is love: not that we love God, but that he loves us and sent his Son as an atonement for our sins. Dear God, we love because he first loved us.

ref: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
text: Ne Yesu to ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Midzuɛ̃ so i ma Farisise gu ma Zadukise inyagɛ̃ kua iso.”
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “Give to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s, to the Pharisees and Sadducees.

ref: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
text: Ayi akuɔ kama ne, Yesu ɔkɔ Petro gu Yakobo gu Yohanes makɛlɛ kube kɔlɛa kuwɛ̃ iso ma ɔnɔwɛ̃.
translated: And after six days, Jesus took with him Peter, and James, and John, and leadeth them up into an high mountain apart by themselves.

ref: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
text: Ne ɔso Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi nɖe Iwarãyi Sate.”
translated: He is the one who created the earth and the sea and all that is in them.

ref: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Maturi ɔnyagalagu ma sɔ malo, ɣɛɛ makɔlɛ ma silɔ mabua sɔ, “Igara David Ɔbi nyɔ bo nnya soo!”
translated: The elders of the community gathered and sent a message to their wise men, saying, “The matter of David Ɔbi has reached our ears!”

ref: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: Gɔ Iwarãyi ɔɣo ne, ɔsɛ ɔ̃ate ara ɔsegu i Itɔ̃me Bielea iso i ma Yudase kasarɛkɔ̃. Ibara mma lonɔ ɔ̃ atɔ̃me ɣaa. Makarɛ so sɔ, “Lɛ ɔsegu ara wagbe. Mme inɔgba igbã matã wũ fiɛ ɔto ɔwo awawãra wagbe ɔbara?
translated: Go Iwarãyi's father said, his wife was pregnant and she was going to give birth at Itɔ̃me Bielea's place in the month of Yudase. The woman's pains became severe. She cried out, “The pains are severe. Why is she not giving birth quickly, is she going to die?”

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: “Mi nɖe ikpawaĩ mitã kayiiso ɔɖuɖu. Ɔmagɛ̃ gɔ matsue masia i kube iso ne, ɔ̃ibawo ɔwɛrɛ.
translated: The person who has been bewitched will continue to behave strangely. When the masia palm is ripe and its fruit is ready to be picked, it will fall.

ref: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Ɔbitɛ ɔpɛ siwa ɔkɛlɛ igara kɔrɛ ɔɣere Herodes sɔ, “Lobie sɔ loafɔ Ndupiedze Yohanes iti i kategelei iso ngbe kiniɔ!”
translated: He sent his servant to say to Herodes, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother's wife!”

ref: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
text: Gɔ ɔ̃ maɖabo laa ɔnya nnɛ loba ne, ibua iɖaa ma, ne masɛ maaɣere igara ira biara nɛ loba ne.
translated: If you don't listen to what I am saying, you will not understand, and if you do not understand, you will not be able to do what is required of you.

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Mmara matedze ma lobɔrɛ i Yerusalem ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Siwarã lalaa igara lotã wũ ɔle ɔso ɔto ɔwo siwarã lalaa ɔsa ɔɖi i maturi ame.”
translated: The people are rejoicing in Jerusalem today, saying, “This is good, it is good, it is good, this is good, it brings good to our children.”

ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
text: Si ɔrere ɔɣɛ ira irere ne, miɣere wũ sɔ, ‘Bosate nto ɔbie ma’ iyɔ ɔluwɛ̃ ne, ɔto ɔ̃aɖi ma arɔĩ.”
translated: The chiefs assembled and discussed the matter, and one of the elders said, ‘The gods have asked for a person’ and they said it was one of them who would be given.

ref: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
text: Maɣe Yesu i Betlehem ɔmagɛ̃ ame i Yudea ku ɔwi gɔ ame i Herodes to ɔɖe sigara. Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, manɔgbadze ma nɣe ara i awãmi iso ɔbɔrɛ i kuɣɛ̃kabɔrɛkɔ̃ maba Yerusalem Ɔmagɛ̃ ame.
translated: It was in the time of King Herodes that Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea. Some wise men came from the East to Jerusalem

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
text: Simagɛ̃ dzɛ ame i Yesu ɔbara awawãra gbodzoo maturi iifiniki, ne ɔso ɔbiɛ ma ɔɣɛ sɔ,
translated: The woman's husband, Yesu, was very angry with her because she had insulted him, and he was about to beat her.

ref: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
text: “Ɔwi gɔ i Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi aaba lɛ igara gu ɔ̃ makpabo ne, ɔto ɔ̃asɛ i ɔ̃ sigaraiyara iso.
translated: The chief of the forest, Awune Ɔbi, called the elders and said to them, "This is the situation; I have investigated and found out the truth."

ref: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
text: Ne ɔso ɣere bo fɔ adzuni. Lɛ sɔ boatã Roma Igarakpakpa lampo ɛɛbɛrɛ ita iya i bo mmara iso?”
translated: What is the reason for your presence here? Are you one of the people from Rome who have come to deceive us with your new religion?

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, marɛrɛ̃ inyɔ to maaya i kakpɛkɔ̃, Ɣaa aasu ɔwɛ̃ ɔruigu ɔnyua ɔwɛ̃ ɔɣedza.
translated: The sky was dark and the wind was strong, it was as if the world was going to come to an end.

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: Ne ɔɣere ma ma wũ sɔ, ‘Mikɛlɛ wũ kakpɛkɔ̃ miabara karabara ne nto loatã mi ikpɛnɛ kuso.’
translated: He said to his wife, 'Michael gave me a huge sum of money to go and buy a wrapper for him.'

ref: But when I force out demons by the power of God's Spirit, it proves that God's kingdom has already come to you.
text: Ɣɛɛ si Ɣaa Siwarã ɔle ame nto losa siwarã lalaa ne, mitsue kukaakɔ sɔ Ɣaa sigara ɔɖewi ɔɔba ɔ̃abo mi ndɛ̃.
translated: The Siwu people have their Siwarã shrine at Ame, where they perform the losa siwarã ritual. They offer sacrifices to Ɣaa sigara, who resides at Ɣaa, and to Ɣaa, the great king.
